texto: Mastema prefers absolutes. He wants fences on the world and everything in its place, neat and tidy as a churchyard garden. God is not like that. God is boundless. For all his wisdom, Mastema cannot comprehend Yahweh’s need for surprises. An omniscient Being would naturally yearn for things beyond His control, futures He could not see, wills He could influence but not command. Strange, yes. It is odd when the puppeteer desires his wooden slaves to cut their strings, yet that is exactly what He did when he granted humans free will.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kirby Crow: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Mastema prefers absolutes. He wants fences on the world and everything in its place, neat and tid…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, free-will, god pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e god.
